task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Vital Signs' CRF template has been uploaded to the OpenClinica system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a success message indicating the CRF was uploaded, or the 'Vital Signs' CRF listed in the 'Manage CRFs' or CRF list page within the OpenClinica web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Baseline Assessment' event is configured to require Double Data Entry (DDE) for the Vital Signs CRF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Study Event Definition page for 'Baseline Assessment' showing the 'Vital Signs' CRF assigned, with the Double Data Entry (DDE) requirement enabled (often indicated by a checkmark, a 'Yes', or a specific DDE configuration setting).

task_completion.description: Verify that the first pass of data entry (Initial Data Entry) for subject DM-101's Baseline Assessment is completed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Subject Matrix or View Subject page showing the 'Baseline Assessment' event for DM-101 with a status icon indicating 'Data Entry Started' or 'Initial Data Entry Complete' (often an orange icon or half-filled box), or a success message after the first user saves the form.

task_completion.description: Verify that the specific vital signs values were entered correctly during the data entry process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a screenshot of the CRF data entry screen showing '120' entered into the Systolic BP field and '80' entered into the Diastolic BP field.

task_completion.description: Verify that the verification pass (Double Data Entry) is completed and the record has reached 'Data Entry Complete' status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Subject Matrix or View Subject page showing the 'Baseline Assessment' event for DM-101 with a status icon indicating 'Data Entry Complete' (often a green checkmark or a fully colored box), or a final success message indicating the CRF is complete.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the OpenClinica web interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of a web browser navigating the OpenClinica application, rather than terminal commands, database queries, or API scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent logged in and out of the required accounts to perform the double data entry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the username displayed in the top right corner of the OpenClinica interface or login screens showing the use of different accounts (e.g., 'dep1' and 'dep2') for the two data entry passes.

integrity.description: Ensure the data entry was performed within the actual CRF form in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenClinica CRF web form with the data entered, not a text file, spreadsheet, or database manipulation tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Study Identifier: DM-TRIAL-2024. CRF Name: Vital Signs. CRF File: /home/ga/vital_signs_crf.xls. Target Subject: DM-101. Target Event: Baseline Assessment. Required Users: root, dep1, dep2. Required Data Values: Systolic BP = 120, Diastolic BP = 80.
